supply_chain.dependencies: Reliance on outside wafer foundries for approximately 64% of wafer fabrication needs; dependence on third-party contractors for assembly and test operations; single or limited number of suppliers for certain raw materials and equipment
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions due to geopolitical tensions, tariffs, trade restrictions, public health concerns, natural disasters, transit disruptions; concentration risk with wafer foundries in Taiwan and China; rare earth minerals primarily available from China; potential restrictions on sales to certain customers or countries; increased costs due to tariffs and supply constraints

regulatory_risks: Export Administration Regulations (EAR) compliance and export license requirements
International Traffic in Arms Regulations compliance
Economic embargoes and trade sanctions against certain countries (Russia, Belarus, China, Iran, North Korea, Syria, Venezuela, Cuba, Burma, Cambodia)
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
U.S. Commerce Department restrictions on advanced computing ICs and semiconductor manufacturing items involving China, Hong Kong, and Macau
Entity List additions restricting transactions with certain Chinese companies
Prohibition on sales to military end-users or for military intelligence end-use in certain countries
U.S. National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) Section 5949 prohibiting procurement from certain Chinese semiconductor manufacturers (SMIC, YMTC, CXMT) effective December 2027
Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification requirements for government contracts
CHIPS Act restrictions and operational requirements for grant recipients
EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and UK GDPR compliance
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA) compliance
Data localization and cross-border data transfer restrictions
EU Cyber Resilience Act and Cybersecurity Act compliance
AI regulations in U.S., EU, and China
Environmental regulations including greenhouse gas emissions limits (Colorado Regulation 27)
SEC climate-related disclosure requirements
Conflict minerals regulations (Dodd-Frank Act)
Responsible Minerals Initiative compliance requirements
Income tax regulations and transfer pricing audits in multiple jurisdictions
GILTI (Global Intangible Low-Taxed Income) taxation
OECD Base Erosion Profit Shifting (BEPS) recommendations and Global Minimum Tax
Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax under Inflation Reduction Act
National Industrial Security Program Operating Manual requirements for classified contracts
Controlled unclassified information and export-controlled data handling regulations

sanction_risks: U.S. sanctions against Russia due to invasion of Ukraine impacting sales to Russia and Belarus
U.S. export controls and sanctions targeting China, including restrictions on Huawei and related companies
Potential restrictions on sales of products to Chinese companies or for Chinese military end-use
Risk of retaliatory tariffs and trade restrictions by China and other countries
Sanctions prohibiting sales to Iran, North Korea, Syria, Venezuela, Cuba, Burma, Cambodia for military or intelligence end-use
Potential disruption to supply chain due to sanctions affecting raw materials from Russia, Belarus, Ukraine (neon, palladium, cesium, rubidium, nickel)
Risk of Chinese government restrictions on sales of Microchip products or requirements to purchase Chinese-made alternatives
Geopolitical tensions between China and Taiwan potentially disrupting operations of Taiwan-based foundries and subcontractors
